spark-gluten:position-deletes:v2.notes: Gluten/Velox accelerates reads only; all writes and DML fall back to vanilla Spark, which provides the underlying feature support.
caveats: Writes/DML are executed by vanilla Spark, not the native engine

spark-gluten:position-deletes:v3.notes: Gluten/Velox accelerates reads only; all writes and DML fall back to vanilla Spark, which provides the underlying feature support.
caveats: Writes/DML are executed by vanilla Spark, not the native engine
Deletion vectors (V3) require Iceberg 1.6+ and Spark 3.5+ or 4.0+

spark-gluten:equality-deletes:v2.notes: Gluten/Velox accelerates reads only; all writes and DML fall back to vanilla Spark, which provides the underlying feature support.

spark-gluten:copy-on-write:v3.notes: Gluten/Velox accelerates reads only; all writes and DML fall back to vanilla Spark, which provides the underlying feature support.
caveats: Writes/DML are executed by vanilla Spark, not the native engine

spark-gluten:schema-evolution:v2.notes: Read path is natively accelerated by the Gluten Velox backend (offloaded scans for Parquet and ORC). Partitioned-table scans, metadata-table reads, equality-delete reads, and unsupported expressions fall back to vanilla Spark. Iceberg feature support is inherited from Spark.
caveats: Avro is not offloaded; partitioned tables and metadata reads mostly fall back to vanilla Spark

spark-gluten:schema-evolution:v3.notes: Read path is natively accelerated by the Gluten Velox backend (offloaded scans for Parquet and ORC). Partitioned-table scans, metadata-table reads, equality-delete reads, and unsupported expressions fall back to vanilla Spark. Iceberg feature support is inherited from Spark.
caveats: Avro is not offloaded; partitioned tables and metadata reads mostly fall back to vanilla Spark

spark-gluten:column-default-values:v2.notes: Column default values (initial-default, write-default) are a V3 feature

spark-gluten:column-default-values:v3.notes: The Velox native scan does not yet accelerate this V3 type; reads fall back to vanilla Spark, which provides the underlying support.
caveats: Reads of this type fall back to vanilla Spark (no native Velox acceleration yet)
No Spark SQL DDL syntax for setting Iceberg column defaults
Requires Iceberg 1.6+ with format-version 3

spark-gluten:nanosecond-timestamps:v2.notes: Nanosecond timestamps are a V3 feature

spark-gluten:nanosecond-timestamps:v3.notes: The Velox native scan does not yet accelerate this V3 type; reads fall back to vanilla Spark, which provides the underlying support.
caveats: Reads of this type fall back to vanilla Spark (no native Velox acceleration yet)
Microsecond TIMESTAMP remains the default; only switch when nanosecond precision is required
Requires format-version 3

Partitioned-table scans, metadata-table reads, equality-delete reads, and unsupported expressions fall back to vanilla Spark. Iceberg feature support is inherited from Spark.

caveats: Writes/DML are executed by vanilla Spark, not the native engine
Requires Iceberg SQL extensions

caveats: Reads of this type fall back to vanilla Spark (no native Velox acceleration yet)
Multi-source-column transforms are a new V3 capability with evolving Spark support
Requires Spark 4.0+ for full V3 transform support

Bloom filters must be explicitly enabled per column via table properties
Not enabled by default
